source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the main CFRP fuselage truss frame of a 25 kg-class light unmanned aircraft per EASA SC Light-UAS 01 (2020), delivered as a multi-part assembly because a truss frame is by construction a built-up structure whose maneuver, landing, wing-attach, and crashworthiness pass/fail all reference the coupled behavior of longerons, transverse bulkheads, and diagonal shear braces connected by adhesive bonds and mechanical fasteners at wing and engine mounts. Applicable clauses: SC Light-UAS.2255 (n +3.8 / -1.52 VLA-equivalent default), SC Light-UAS.2270 (ultimate = 1.5 x limit), SC Light-UAS.2310 (crashworthiness energy absorption).

Assembly layout. (A) Longerons: 6 carbon-epoxy pultruded UD tubes, 20 mm OD, 2 mm wall, running the full 2000 mm fuselage length. (B) Transverse bulkheads: 8 CFRP quasi-isotropic plates 150 mm x 120 mm x 3 mm layup [0/+45/-45/90]_s, spaced along the fuselage to partition the truss. (C) Diagonal shear braces: CFRP tubes or flat members connecting longerons and bulkheads to provide shear stiffness. (D) Wing-attach fasteners (x4) at top bulkhead and engine-mount fasteners (x4) at nose; all other joints are adhesive structural epoxy. Payload bay 600 mm x 250 mm x 200 mm at CG. Frame envelope 2000 mm x 300 mm x 250 mm.

Material. Longeron T700/epoxy pultruded UD: E_L = 120 GPa, E_T = 9 GPa, rho = 1550 kg/m^3, Xt = 1800 MPa, Xc = 1100 MPa. Bulkhead QI [0/+45/-45/90]_s: E = 52 GPa, tensile 650 MPa, compression 480 MPa, shear 110 MPa. Composite ultimate knockdown 1.25 (environment + scatter) => effective Xt = 1440 MPa, Xc = 880 MPa, bulkhead tension 520 MPa, compression 384 MPa.

Service envelope. MTOM 25 kg, VC = 40 m/s, VD = 55 m/s, VS = 18 m/s. n_pos = +3.8, n_neg = -1.52, ultimate 1.5x. Crash impact V = 15 m/s giving E_crash = 0.5 * 25 * 15^2 = 2812 J. Peak payload-bay deceleration <= 20 g proxy; if uniform 2812 J over 300 mm stroke gives 38 g, stroke must extend to >= 573 mm or controlled-failure stages added.

Load cases. LC1 (pos maneuver ultimate +5.7 g): 1957 N at payload-bay CG. LC2 (neg maneuver ultimate -2.28 g): -783 N. LC3 (landing 3 g ultimate): 1030 N vertical at gear. LC4 (wing-attach ultimate): 560 N at wing attach bulkhead. LC5 (crashworthiness): 2812 J absorbed progressively via longeron crush.

Pass/fail criteria (all on the assembled truss). (R1) Longeron fiber stress ultimate tension (LC1/LC2/LC4) <= Xt/1.25 = 1440 MPa. (R2) Bulkhead in-plane stress ultimate tension (LC1-LC3) <= 520 MPa. (R3) Longeron compression ultimate (LC1/LC2) <= Xc/1.25 = 880 MPa. (R4) Wing-attach interface longeron stress (LC4) <= 1440 MPa. (R5) Crash LC5 energy >= 2812 J absorbed with peak payload accel <= 20 g proxy. (R6) Frame mass <= 2.8 kg. (R7) Progressive composite failure ordering: no mechanical joint (wing/engine fasteners) may fail before the longeron progressive failure under LC5 (binary toughening-priority gate).

Assembly policy. Every LC requires the assembled truss: longeron stresses (R1, R3) depend on bulkhead spacing acting as intermediate supports; R2 is an interface stress where fasteners or bonds enter bulkheads; R4 is a fastener/longeron interface stress; R5 is an assembly-level energy dissipation; R7 explicitly compares joint failure to longeron failure.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary: linear static FEM for LC1-LC4 (composite-shell/continuum-shell). Explicit dynamic crash FEM (LS-DYNA or Abaqus Explicit) or a closed-form energy-absorption calc for LC5. Modal, thermal, fluid, ionizing-radiation, separate-buckling NOT required.

Deliverables. STEP AP242 of truss frame, frame mass in kg, max longeron / bulkhead stress per LC, LC5 energy absorbed in J, crush stroke in mm, and peak payload-bay deceleration in g.
short_prompt:
Design the main CFRP fuselage truss frame of a 25 kg-class light UAS per EASA SC Light-UAS 01 (2020), delivered as a built-up multi-part assembly: six T700/epoxy UD pultruded longerons (20 mm OD, 2 mm wall, full 2000 mm length) + eight quasi-isotropic [0/+45/-45/90]_s CFRP bulkheads (150 x 120 x 3 mm) + CFRP diagonal shear braces + four wing-attach fasteners (top bulkhead) + four engine-mount fasteners (nose) + structural-epoxy bonds elsewhere. Payload bay 600 x 250 x 200 mm at CG; frame envelope 2000 x 300 x 250 mm. Applicable: SC Light-UAS.2255 (n +3.8 / -1.52), .2270 (ultimate 1.5x), .2310 (crashworthiness).

Materials. Longerons: E_L = 120 GPa, E_T = 9 GPa, rho = 1550 kg/m^3, Xt = 1800 MPa, Xc = 1100 MPa. Bulkheads (QI): E = 52 GPa, tensile 650 MPa, compression 480 MPa, shear 110 MPa. Composite knockdown 1.25 => Xt_eff = 1440 MPa, Xc_eff = 880 MPa, bulkhead tension 520 MPa, compression 384 MPa.

Service envelope. MTOM 25 kg, VC = 40 m/s, VD = 55 m/s, VS = 18 m/s. Crash V = 15 m/s, E_crash = 2812 J; peak payload-bay decel <= 20 g proxy (requires crush stroke >= 573 mm).

Load cases.
- LC1 pos maneuver ultimate +5.7 g: 1957 N at payload CG.
- LC2 neg maneuver ultimate -2.28 g: -783 N.
- LC3 landing 3 g ultimate: 1030 N at gear.
- LC4 wing-attach ultimate: 560 N at wing-attach bulkhead.
- LC5 crashworthiness: 2812 J absorbed via longeron crush.

Frame mass <= 2.8 kg.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: SC Light-UAS.2310 crashworthiness is a kinetic-energy-absorption problem.

prompt.title: 25 kg light UAS CFRP fuselage truss frame per EASA SC Light-UAS as multi-part assembly
prompt.brief: Pultruded CFRP longerons + 8 QI bulkheads + diagonal shear braces + wing/engine mount fasteners; n +3.8/-1.52 maneuver, ultimate 1.5x; 15 m/s crash with payload accel <=20 g.

parts.name: longeron
parts.role: pultruded CFRP UD tube running fuselage length, primary bending/axial member

parts.name: bulkhead
parts.role: transverse CFRP QI plate partitioning the truss and reacting concentrated loads

parts.name: diagonal_shear_brace
parts.role: diagonal CFRP member providing shear stiffness between longerons and bulkheads

parts.name: mount_fastener
parts.role: mechanical fastener at wing attach or engine mount (4 each)

boundary_conditions.nose: engine mount fixed in static; free-free in crash explicit

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: 0.5*m*V^2 with m=25 kg, V=15 m/s; payload accel <= 20 g proxy
Progressive composite failure before brittle mechanical-joint failure

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: composite shell + continuum shell for crash

interfaces.load_path: longeron bending reacted at bulkhead stations; bulkhead in-plane load from longeron passthrough
shear stiffness and crash-stage load redistribution

interfaces.mediated_by: bolted joint (wing attach or engine mount)
interfaces.load_path: external wing/engine loads into truss via concentrated fastener bearing

fem_check_policy.rationale: Longeron and bulkhead stresses (R1-R4), crash energy absorption (R5), and joint-failure ordering (R7) all reference the assembled truss with longerons supported at bulkheads and braced diagonally; wing-attach stresses depend on the D-B-A chain. No part in isolation has a meaningful pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part_is_inherent: EASA SC Light-UAS crashworthiness and maneuver cases are frame-level; SC.2310 explicitly requires progressive deformation across the assembled airframe.

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Lift already reduced to inertia load.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Longeron local buckling production concern; not in this brief.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: BVID / damage tolerance per composite handbook

eval_coverage: SC Light-UAS maneuver envelope on assembled truss
crashworthiness energy absorption across full assembly
failure-ordering gate spanning bonded and mechanical joints
